Card Name: Globalize SVG Background
Card Description:
## Issues

 Look to investigate this problem and timebox the effort. `expo-system-ui` might be a good place to start looking.

Card Name: User shouldn't be allowed to checkout if Biggby Card has insufficient funds
Card Description:
## Summary
 

 If user processes Biggby card payment with insufficient funds, the Oops! modal displays, with the option to Try Again or Take me home.
 

 ## Steps to Reproduce
 

 1. Log into the app with a user who has a Biggby gift balance < $5
 2. Go through the process to set store if needed
 3. Add an item to the cart
 4. Tap checkout icon
 5. Tap Continue to pickup
 6. Tap Continue to payment
 7. Tap Biggby Card
 8. Tap Checkout. Error displays
 

 ## Expected Behavior
 

 - User shouldn’t be able to tap “Checkout” button if they’re selected on BIGGBY card payment method but there’s not enough in account to cover their order total
 

 ## Screenshots/videos
 

 [Oops for issue procesisng order - insufficient Biggby funds.MP4](https://trello.com/1/cards/689de733cad991a4303264c2/attachments/689de87362ef9a7f7660e2b7/download/Oops_for_issue_procesisng_order_-_insufficient_Biggby_funds.MP4 ""‌")[Oops for issue procesisng order - insufficient Biggby funds.MP4](https://trello.com/1/cards/689de733cad991a4303264c2/attachments/689de87bfbe9eccfb19d5a34/download/Oops_for_issue_procesisng_order_-_insufficient_Biggby_funds.MP4 ""‌")

Card Name: User can access helpful links & signout
Card Description:
## Context
 

 This story is for the external links & login/logout, not for the more in-depth sections of the hamburger menu.
 

 ## Acceptance Criteria
 

 - When user clicks “Help” they are taken to [**https://feedback.bi ggby.com/support/home**](https://feedback.xn--biggby-jk68a.com/support/home ""‌")**,** opening externally in their device’s default browser
 - When user clicks “Privacy Policy” they are taken to [https://www.biggby.com/privacy-policy/](https://www.biggby.com/privacy-policy/ ""‌") opening externally in their device’s default browser
 - When user clicks “Terms & Conditions” they are taken to [https://www.biggby.com/terms-of-service/](https://www.biggby.com/terms-of-service/ ""‌") opening externally in their device’s default browser
 - If a user is currently authenticated, they see “Sign out”
  - If they click “Sign out” their account is signed out and they are returned to the app Home/Dashboard screen in unauthenticated mode
 - If a user is NOT currently authenticated, they see “Sign in”
  - If they click “Sign in” they are taken to the login screen
 - Feel free to add the menu items to the other sections, but they’re not expected to work as part of this story.
 

 **Not included**
 

 - Profile
 - Favorites
 - Cards
 - Orders
 

 ## Tech Hypothesis
 

 - this can just be a screen for now, doesn’t need to be a fancy component
 - Look at React Navigation Drawer, and if it’s super easy just do that
 

 ## Designs
 

 Figma link: [https://www.figma.com/design/3X0u1w0uv66vgc4Ih7awuW/Mobile-App-Dev?node-id=408-5743&t=Rzmi1DbyHeje66lh-4](https://www.figma.com/design/3X0u1w0uv66vgc4Ih7awuW/Mobile-App-Dev?node-id=408-5743&t=Rzmi1DbyHeje66lh-4 "smartCard-inline")

Card Name: User can add balance to BIGGBY card
Card Description:
## Context
 

 This story assumes [https://trello.com/c/wc8RYvFu/56-user-can-manage-cards](https://trello.com/c/wc8RYvFu/56-user-can-manage-cards "smartCard-inline") is complete, and includes the ability to add balance to a card
 

 ## Acceptance Criteria
 

 - When user clicks “Add Balance” button on the Single card screen, take them to a new screen where we display card number, current balance and a calculation of what the new balance will be
 - User can pick from specified dollar amounts, or choose custom to define their own. Specified dollar amounts include $5, $10, $20, $25
  - When user chooses custom, they can type in the dollar amount they’d like to add to balance. This number must be an integer between 1 and XX.
 - User can use either a credit card or mobile pay (Apple Pay / Google Pay) to process their payment
 - If user picks Credit Card, display the necessary fields for the API to accept the payment
 - If user picks the mobile pay option, they are told “Another step will appear to securely submit your payment information.”
 - When the user clicks Save, the transaction is submitted and they are returned to the page they started from
 

 ## Tech Hypothesis
 

 - For the custom dollar amount, XX is whatever max value the API will accept … because in current app Renee managed to get an api error saying “Variable $amount got invalid value 10000000000000; int cannot represent non 32-bit signed integer value: 10000000000000” when she tried to add $10000000000000 to a gift card
 - Credit card and mobile pay (Apple Pay / Google Pay) are handled via Stripe behind the scenes at BIGGBY
 

 ## Designs
 

 - [Add balance initial screen](https://www.figma.com/design/3X0u1w0uv66vgc4Ih7awuW/Mobile-App-Dev?node-id=860-17831&t=AVZjsOAA94tU966E-4 ""‌")
 - [Single card mock](https://www.figma.com/design/3X0u1w0uv66vgc4Ih7awuW/Mobile-App-Dev?node-id=510-7629&t=AVZjsOAA94tU966E-4 ""‌")

Card Name: User can view store menu even if not accepting online orders (just can't order)
Card Description:
## Context
 

 We got the following beta user feedback:
 

 > can't view the menu when stores are closed 
 > locations don't show up when stores aren't open
 

 Jake & Brianna would like us to support viewing menu & products even while stores are closed, but prevent them from actually ordering. After chatting with Brianna, this also means our differences between viewing stores on Store tab & Order tab will disappear, so there’s likely some logic code [from past work](https://trello.com/c/KFowLk1q ""‌") that can be simplified.
 

 ## Acceptance Criteria
 

 - All stores (regardless of whether they’re open, closed, or whether they’re accepting online orders or not) should now be selectable anytime a user sees the store selection bottomsheet
 - When a user taps any store, they now see a button to “Select store”. When they tap that button, it takes them to their expected destination (based on what they had interacted with before we forced them to choose a store)
  - This button replaces the “Order here” button that currently exists for stores that are accepting online orders
 - If a user attempts to add a product to their cart while their store is currently closed or not accepting online orders, do not add the product to cart and instead show error dialog
 

 ## Tech Hypothesis
 

 ## Designs
 

 Re-use the error dialog from [https://trello.com/c/MsacKQna](https://trello.com/c/MsacKQna "smartCard-inline")

Card Name: Implement Refresh Token Flow
Card Description:
## Context
 

 Currently, the app supports user login, but it does not handle refreshing the user's token. As a result, when a token expires, the user is treated as if they are logged in but is unable to see their profile/info. To improve the user experience we should implement token refreshing.
 

 ## Acceptance Criteria
 

 - The user can continue to see a their content as long as they have a valid refresh token.
 - Upon app initialization, the app checks for the presence of tokens and confirms they are valid before sending the user to the home screen
 - If a user’s refresh token expire or are revoked at any point, they should be redirect to the “Get Started” screen
 - When new tokens are obtained, they are stored in Secure Storage
 

 ### Out of Scope
 

 - Displaying the a reason for why the user was directed to the “Get Started” screen.
 

 ## Tech Hypothesis
 

 Look to build a Biggby API client that can automatically detect an invalid token, attempt to refresh it, and reissue the original request.
 

 Ensure this client is used in `services` that interact with the Biggby api

  - “SAVE YOUR BIGGBY LOCATIONS! Quickly order from your favorite BIGGBY locations with a tap of a finger. To save your locations, sign up for a BIGGBY rewards membership today!”
 - When a user who’s not logged in clicks the “favorite” icon on a product screen, show a modal that contains marketing header and text about saving products if you sign up
  - “SAVE YOUR BIGGBY PRODUCTS! Quickly order your favorite BIGGBY food and drink items with a tap of a finger. To save your products, sign up for a BIGGBY rewards membership today!”
 - If a user clicks “Sign up”, take them to the in-app registration screen
 - If a user clicks “Log in”, take them to the login screen
 - If the user clicks the modal “x” close out and return them to the screen they were on

Card Name: Create Reusable Page Header
Card Description:
## Context
 

 Currently we create a new header for each page. The common header types are two-child and three-child headers. The two-child header typically contains the back button and a centered header. The three-child header typically contains the back button, a centered header, and an icon (trash can for the cart page). We want to create a new header component that can be reused when a page is created that needs a header.

Card Name: Set app to light mode by default
Card Description:
## Context
 

 The application currently sets its theme based on the user’s theme at first run (it sets the value into storage). For now, we’d like it to always use light mode.
 

 ## Acceptance Criteria
 

 - The app defaults to light mode regardless of the user’s mode
 

 ## Tech Hypothesis
 

 See `useInitializeApp`. We could remove the behavior entirely or always set light mode into storage. Setting light mode into storage is likely more flexible long term. In order to make the change work for people who have the app installed, change the storage key (also make it constant)

Card Name: User can checkout with credit card or mobile pay
Card Description:
## Context
 

 Assumes [https://trello.com/c/3mszeRby/94-user-can-see-checkout-bottomsheet-w-order-total](https://trello.com/c/3mszeRby/94-user-can-see-checkout-bottomsheet-w-order-total "smartCard-inline") is completed
 

 Don’t focus much on the UI design – Layout may be changing in refined designs. This story is intended to be ensure we can get the functionality to work.
 

 We’re going to be using Stripe-provided payment sheet for this, rather than a custom design.
 

 ## Acceptance Criteria
 

 - When the user looks at the bottom of the cart screen, they now see the option to pay with a credit card or mobile pay in the Payment Method section
 - When the user selects the “Credit Card or Mobile Pay” button, they see the Stripe bottomsheet appear
  - If the user is on an iOS device that supports Mobile Pay, they see “Apple Pay” button available
  - If the user is on an Android device that supports Mobile Pay, they see a “Google Pay” button available
  - _Note: Renee hasn’t been able to reproduce this in production app, so if you can’t get it to work, we should split Android mobile pay into separate story_
  - When the user selects the mobile payment button, they see a notice that says “Another step will appear to securely submit your payment information” and the “Pay” button enables for the user.
 - Once all the required fields have been filled out, the user can choose to place the order
 - When user places the order, the order is submitted to BIGGBY
 

 **Not included**
 

 - Any BIGGBY card functionality
 

 ## Tech Hypothesis
 

 Handle stripe payment result: [https://docs.stripe.com/payments/mobile/finalize-payments-on-the-server?platform=react-native&type=payment#set-up-return-url](https://docs.stripe.com/payments/mobile/finalize-payments-on-the-server?platform=react-native&type=payment#set-up-return-url ""‌")
 

 ## Designs
 

 - [Cart view](https://www.figma.com/design/3X0u1w0uv66vgc4Ih7awuW/Mobile-App-Dev?node-id=1910-30866&t=Ni4ZqcKPknHljXhG-4 ""‌")
 - [Selected stripe-type payment](https://www.figma.com/design/3X0u1w0uv66vgc4Ih7awuW/Mobile-App-Dev?node-id=1928-31076&t=Ni4ZqcKPknHljXhG-4 ""‌")

Card Name: User can checkout with BIGGBY card
Card Description:
## Context
 

 Assumes [https://trello.com/c/3mszeRby/94-user-can-see-checkout-bottomsheet-w-order-total](https://trello.com/c/3mszeRby/94-user-can-see-checkout-bottomsheet-w-order-total "smartCard-inline") is completed.
 

 Don’t focus much on the UI design – Layout may be changing in refined designs. This story is intended to be ensure we can get the functionality to work.
 

 ## Acceptance Criteria
 

 - When the user looks at the bottom of the cart screen, they now see the option to pay with a BIGGBY gift card in the Payment Method section
 - When the user clicks the “BIGGBY Card” button, they see a bottomsheet pop up
  - The user’s default card is shown at the top and is the initially selected default payment
  - If a user has multiple BIGGBY cards associated with their account, the other BIGGBY cards are available to select below the default card
  - Each card’s balance displays in a parenthetical next to the card number in the dropdown
 - If more than 4 BIGGBY cards associated with the account, “Show all cards” becomes an option and when clicked, the full list of available BIGGBY cards display on this screen and are selectable
 - If no BIGGBY cards exist on the account, show a message that says “No BIGGBY cards available”
 - Clicking the “X” on the bottomsheet returns the user to the cart screen
 - Once the user has selected a BIGGBY card with a balance that is more than the order total, the “Place Order” button enables for the user
 - When the user clicks “Place Order” the order is submitted to BIGGBY
 

 **Not included**
 

 - Any credit card functionality
 - Any mobile pay functionality
 - Any ability to add to BIGGBY card balance from this screen
 

 ## Tech Hypothesis
 

 - According to Sung Woo, all digital accounts will have at least one BIGGBY card associated with it

Card Name: User can review order in three steps before checking out
Card Description:
## Context
 

 To simplify the amount of information exposed at one time to the user in the cart, we’re breaking the current experience up into three steps:
 

 ## Acceptance Criteria
 

 - The user can see three sections for the checkout process: Cart, Pickup, Payment.
 - Across all three sections, the user can see their subtotal, tax, and order total
 - In section 1 (Cart), the user sees all items currently in cart, their prices, and the size (or bagel type) modifier summary. They can also choose to modify the item, favorite the product, discard the item, or change the quantity
  - If the current quantity is 1, they cannot lower it to 0
 - In section 2 (Pickup), the user sees and can modify the pickup location, pickup person and pickup method
 - In section 3 (Payment), the user can choose between available payment methods. When they click the “Checkout” button it opens the expected payment bottomsheet.
  - On the Payment checkout screen, only show the total account balance instead of the individual cards
 - When user clicks “x” in top left, it closes the cart and takes the user to the home screen
 - User can click the step number to jump to that stage in checkout process
 

 **Not included**
 

 - Accordion-style cards displaying modifiers for product items in cart
 - Suggested add-ons for items in cart
 

 ## Tech Hypothesis
 

 ## Designs
 

 Figma link: [https://www.figma.com/design/3X0u1w0uv66vgc4Ih7awuW/Mobile-App-Dev?node-id=2321-25954&t=Ee2aAQR5cAqKpq7A-4](https://www.figma.com/design/3X0u1w0uv66vgc4Ih7awuW/Mobile-App-Dev?node-id=2321-25954&t=Ee2aAQR5cAqKpq7A-4 "smartCard-inline")
